scope_boundary: Only the bounded synthetic costed-route/resource-budget selection claim moves to synthetic-test-backed: the validator recomputes one public-safe three-route slice, rejects the cheap failed negative control, compares against an adequate overkill baseline, and selects the lowest-cost eligible route while preserving fallback, residual, and non-claim boundaries.
transition_reason: The local validation command checked three Costed Route Record instances and three Resource Budget Record instances against their schemas, recomputed the deterministic cost formula, rejected route://cheap-unverified-transform because verification failed, outcome_state was cheap_brittle, promotion_candidate was false, and the budget was residualized, then selected route://bounded-transform-plus-verifier over the eligible route://frontier-manual-review baseline with a 66.98 percent synthetic cost reduction. This supports only the narrow recorded selector discipline claim, not deployed routing, scheduler behavior, model quality, benchmark quality, or chapter core claims.
negative_results: route://cheap-unverified-transform is cheaper at 2.3 synthetic cost units but rejected because verification_result is fail, outcome_state is cheap_brittle, promotion_candidate is false, budget_state is residualized, and budget_decision is residual

route://frontier-manual-review remains eligible as an adequate overkill baseline at 43.0 synthetic cost units but is not selected because route://bounded-transform-plus-verifier is eligible at 14.2 synthetic cost units
no chapter core claim is promoted above argument
no deployed routing, scheduler, runtime, benchmark, model-quality, source-interpretation, safety, or economic outcome is claimed
downgrade_triggers: validator fails to reject the cheaper failed negative control
selected route is not the lowest-cost eligible route
baseline route is no longer eligible
result record drifts from recomputed cost units
chapter prose interprets this slice as deployed runtime, model, benchmark, scheduler, safety, or economic evidence
promotion_burden: Broader routing or resource-economics claims require a separate accepted transition with real route-quality measurements, deployed scheduler traces, load or serving-system measurements, TokenMana or PlanForge prototype runs, hidden-cost audits over real tasks, independent review where relevant, and explicit non-claims.
limitations: synthetic public-safe route and budget records only
local deterministic validation rather than a deployed router or scheduler
cost formula is a toy accounting rule for this slice, not an economic model
does not measure model quality, benchmark quality, useful-solution performance, load stability, KV-cache behavior, or safety outcomes
does not promote any chapter core claim above argument
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent external audit
non_claims: does not promote any chapter core claim
does not prove deployed routing, scheduler, runtime, load, KV-cache, or economic behavior
does not measure model quality, benchmark quality, useful-solution performance, or safety outcomes
does not prove source interpretation, ASI capability, governance effectiveness, or benchmark performance
does not replace future prototype traces, real route-quality measurements, load tests, benchmark runs, Theseus or Circle imported receipts, or independent review
